svg.text: Features unlocked by transaction detail page
What becomes possible when users tap into a transaction
User writes: "Coffee with Sarah to discuss project"
Searchable, contextual memory. Later: "What did I
spend on meetings?" searches notes + amounts.
Makes expense tracker a personal finance journal,
not just a ledger. Users feel heard.
Tap edit → change amount, category, date, notes.
Great for recurring expenses or quick logging.
Bottom sheet has: "Attach receipt" button. User
snaps photo or picks from gallery. OCR reads
total, date, items. Attached to transaction.
Tax reports: "Show all expenses with receipts
Button: "Split this bill". Tap → Add who was
there, how much each person owes. Creates
I need to split with Sarah" → One tap done.
User realizes: "Oh, I do this every month."
Users don't always know upfront what's recurring.
This feature catches it after logging.
AI tagged as "Dining", user changes to "Business
meal". App learns this correction → improves
Each correction trains the AI to get better.
Bottom sheet lets user add secondary tags:
Later, filter by "Business" to see all business
Freelancers need to separate personal vs
business for taxes. Multi-tag unlocks this.
"Remind me about this next month" → App flags
on same date next year. Great for annual bills
User already looking at transaction. One toggle.
Share button → Screenshot, PDF, or send link
to collaborator. Use case: Roommate owes you
→ Share receipt. Or expense report to boss.
Premium feature: Signed receipts for reimbursement
Detail page shows: "You spent $50 on dining
today. That's 15% above your daily average."
Or: "You bought at Whole Foods 8 times this
month ($780 total). That's 45% of your food
These 3 add 80% of value with 20% effort.
These unlock more engagement and sharing.
